name: Classic Buttermilk Pancakes
steps: Whisk the dry ingredients together in a large bowl.
Whisk buttermilk, eggs, and melted butter in another bowl.
Fold wet into dry until just combined — lumps are fine.
Cook 1/4-cup scoops on a buttered griddle over medium heat.
Flip when bubbles pop on the surface, about 2 minutes per side.

name: Veggie Omelette
description: A quick three-egg omelette loaded with vegetables.
steps: Sauté the pepper, onion, and mushrooms in half the butter until soft.
Beat the eggs with salt and pepper.
Melt remaining butter, pour in eggs, and cook undisturbed 1 minute.
Add veggies and cheese to one half, fold, and cook 1 more minute.

name: Overnight Oats
description: No-cook oats that are ready when you wake up.
steps: Stir everything except the fruit together in a jar.
Cover and refrigerate overnight or at least 4 hours.
Top with fruit and serve cold.

name: French Toast
description: Golden custardy French toast with cinnamon.
steps: Whisk eggs, milk, vanilla, and cinnamon in a shallow dish.
Soak each bread slice 20 seconds per side.
Cook in butter over medium heat until golden, 2-3 minutes per side.
Serve with maple syrup.

name: Chicken Caesar Wrap
description: A crunchy handheld take on the classic salad.
steps: Toss the lettuce, chicken, dressing, parmesan, and croutons together.
Pile the mixture down the center of each tortilla.
Fold in the sides and roll tightly.
Slice in half and serve.

name: Tomato Basil Soup
description: Silky tomato soup that pairs perfectly with grilled cheese.
steps: Soften the onion in olive oil, then add garlic for 1 minute.
Add tomatoes and broth; simmer 20 minutes.
Blend until smooth with the basil.
Stir in cream, season, and serve hot.

name: Grilled Cheese Sandwich
description: Crispy outside, melty inside — the reliable classic.
steps: Butter one side of each bread slice.
Layer the cheeses between the slices, buttered sides out.
Cook in a skillet over medium-low heat until deep golden, about 3-4 minutes per side.
Rest 1 minute before slicing.

name: Chicken Quesadillas
description: Crispy tortillas packed with chicken and melted cheese.
steps: Spread chicken, cheese, and salsa over half of each tortilla.
Fold tortillas over the filling.
Cook in oil over medium heat until crisp, about 3 minutes per side.
Cut into wedges and serve with sour cream.

name: Chicken Parmesan
description: Breaded chicken cutlets baked with marinara and mozzarella.
steps: Dredge cutlets in flour, then egg, then a breadcrumb-parmesan mix.
Pan-fry in oil until golden, about 3 minutes per side.
Top each cutlet with marinara and mozzarella in a baking dish.
Bake at 425°F (220°C) until the cheese melts and bubbles, about 10 minutes.
Serve over spaghetti or with a green salad.

name: Spaghetti Bolognese
description: A rich, slow-simmered meat sauce over pasta.
steps: Brown the beef in olive oil, then remove.
Soften onion and carrots; add garlic and tomato paste for 1 minute.
Return beef with crushed tomatoes; simmer 30-45 minutes.
Stir in the milk near the end and season.
Toss with cooked spaghetti and serve.

name: Sheet Pan Salmon and Vegetables
description: One-pan salmon with roasted broccoli and potatoes.
steps: Roast the potatoes with oil, salt, and pepper at 425°F (220°C) for 15 minutes.
Add broccoli and push everything to the sides.
Place salmon in the center; season with garlic, lemon, salt, and pepper.
Roast 12-15 more minutes until the salmon flakes easily.

name: Beef Tacos
description: Weeknight tacos with seasoned ground beef.
steps: Brown the beef and drain any excess fat.
Stir in the spices with a splash of water; simmer 5 minutes.
Warm the taco shells in the oven.
Fill shells with beef and pile on the toppings.

name: Vegetable Stir-Fry
description: Crisp vegetables in a glossy garlic-ginger sauce.
steps: Stir-fry the vegetables in hot oil until crisp-tender, about 4 minutes.
Add garlic and ginger for 30 seconds.
Whisk soy sauce, honey, and cornstarch with 2 tbsp water; pour in.
Toss until the sauce thickens and coats everything; serve over rice.

name: Homemade Margherita Pizza
description: Simple pizza with tomato, mozzarella, and fresh basil.
steps: Heat the oven to its highest setting with a pizza stone or inverted sheet pan inside.
Stretch the dough to a 12-inch round.
Top with crushed tomatoes, a pinch of salt, and the mozzarella.
Bake 8-12 minutes until blistered and bubbling.
Finish with basil and a drizzle of olive oil.

name: Banana Smoothie
description: A creamy anytime smoothie with three ingredients.
steps: Add everything to a blender.
Blend until completely smooth, about 45 seconds.
Pour and serve immediately.

name: Garlic Butter Rice
description: A fragrant side dish that goes with everything.
steps: Toast the garlic in butter over medium heat until fragrant.
Stir in the rice to coat, then add broth and a pinch of salt.
Cover and simmer on low 18 minutes.
Rest 5 minutes, fluff, and fold in the parsley.
